active_projects.description: EXPERIMENTAL: Develop an alternative completeness proof that eliminates BFMCS bundle machinery entirely. Define canonical model with box-class equivalence for modal accessibility directly on worlds (MCS, time) pairs, not families. Use box_theory_witness_exists for the Box truth lemma without bundle quantification. Develop independently until proven to work, then consider replacing existing infrastructure. Believe it when I see it approach.

Eliminate the custom axiom discrete_Icc_finite_axiom (FrameConditions/Completeness.lean line 187). Either prove the finiteness of DiscreteTimelineQuot intervals directly, or restructure the discrete completeness proof to avoid needing it. Research-heavy task.

Fill 5 sorries in Soundness.lean for frame-specific axiom validity: density (line 572), discreteness_forward (line 576), seriality_future (line 579), seriality_past (line 582), temporal_duality (line 602). These require frame-specific proofs using DenselyOrdered, SuccOrder constraints.

Connect construct_bfmcs to the top-level completeness theorems in FrameConditions/Completeness.lean. Eliminate the 3 sorries: dense_completeness_fc (line 108), discrete_completeness_fc (line 151), completeness_over_Int (line 170). This wires the sorry-free algebraic path through to the final completeness statements.

Clean up UltrafilterChain.lean: remove ~150 lines of unused Phase 1 ultrafilter relations (R_G, R_Box, R_G_refl, R_G_trans, R_Box_refl, R_Box_euclidean, R_Box_symm, R_Box_trans) that are never referenced by the actual box-class construction. Remove ~280 lines of verbose exploratory comments in box_class_witness_consistent. Consider renaming file to BoxClassBFMCS.lean to match what it actually does.

Remove ~2500 lines of dead code from SuccChainFMCS.lean. Delete deprecated approaches: restricted_succ_propagates_F_not variants (9 sorries, proven FALSE), single_step_forcing attempts, f_nesting_is_bounded/p_nesting_is_bounded (deprecated), fuel-based and boundary-resolution approaches. Also remove p_step_blocking_for_deferral_restricted in RestrictedMCS.lean.

Prove SuccChain temporal coherence directly, bypassing f_nesting_is_bounded. Replace the sorry chain (f_nesting_is_bounded → f_nesting_boundary → succ_chain_forward_F → SuccChainTemporalCoherent) with a direct argument using Succ obligation resolution. Supersedes tasks 36, 37, 53.

active_projects.description: Prove f_nesting_boundary axiom (SuccChainFMCS.lean:583) via temporal filtration or Fischer-Ladner closure. The axiom states: given F(phi) in MCS M, there exists d >= 1 such that iter_F d phi in M but iter_F (d+1) phi not in M. Requires showing F-chains in consistent MCS must terminate. Standard proof uses Fischer-Ladner closure finiteness — the closure of any formula is finite, so the F-iteration sequence must eventually leave M. This eliminates the axiom entirely.

artifacts.summary: Analyzed f_nesting_boundary axiom. Key finding: proof requires showing F-iterations eventually leave MCS via Nat.find with classical existence proof.
5-phase implementation plan to eliminate f_nesting_boundary and p_nesting_boundary axioms via iter_F complexity lemmas and Nat.find extraction
Converted f_nesting_boundary and p_nesting_boundary axioms to theorems with localized sorries in helper lemmas

active_projects.description: Prove p_nesting_boundary axiom (SuccChainFMCS.lean:695) via temporal filtration or Fischer-Ladner closure. Symmetric to f_nesting_boundary: given P(phi) in MCS M, there exists d >= 1 such that iter_P d phi in M but iter_P (d+1) phi not in M. Once f_nesting_boundary (task 36) is proven, this follows by F/P duality with minimal additional work. Depends on task 36 infrastructure.

Resolves the Task 988 blocker via the DenseTask framework.

active_projects.description: Replace FlagBFMCS satisfies_at with a canonical TaskFrame using truth_at. The current FlagBFMCS completeness theorem uses an internal satisfies_at relation rather than the official truth_at from the semantic layer. Eliminate satisfies_at entirely and construct a canonical TaskFrame directly from FlagBFMCS data, so that the completeness theorem states: consistent S implies existence of a TaskModel and world where truth_at holds for all φ ∈ S. Key architectural requirements: (1) Define canonical world states from CanonicalMCS, (2) Define the duration domain D parametrically (positions within each Flag chain serve as durations), (3) Define the task relation R on world states using CanonicalR, (4) Construct WorldHistory instances from Flags (each Flag yields a history mapping durations to world states), (5) Define the canonical TaskFrame and TaskModel using these components, (6) Prove the truth lemma directly for truth_at rather than satisfies_at, (7) State and prove the completeness theorem using the canonical valid predicate. The existing FlagBFMCS infrastructure (closedFlags, chainFMCS, modal saturation, temporal completeness) provides all the raw material. The work is restructuring it to fit the official TaskFrame/TaskModel/WorldHistory architecture. This supersedes the validity bridge approach in task 997.

active_projects.description: Redesign the FMP filtration for strict temporal semantics. The 2 sorry theorems in Decidability/FMP/TruthPreservation.lean — mcs_all_future_closure (line 263) and mcs_all_past_closure (line 281) — are deprecated because the temporal T-axiom (Gphi -> phi) is NOT valid under strict semantics. filtration_all_future_forward and filtration_all_past_forward depend on them. The FMP module is separate from the main decidability pipeline (decide is sorry-free), but completing it formally proves the finite model property. Resolution options: (A) restrict FMP statement to serial frames where temporal seriality holds, (B) redesign filtration to avoid temporal reflexivity entirely, (C) prove the filtered model satisfies a weaker correctness property sufficient for the FMP theorem. Note: mcs_finite_model_property in FMP.lean does NOT directly use these sorry lemmas, so the impact is localized to filtration_all_future_forward/backward.

Implement the Shift-Closed Tense S5 Algebra (STSA) representation theorem. Define STSA as a Lean structure extending BooleanAlgebra with □, G, H, σ operators and interaction axioms. Lift temporal duality σ from swap_temporal to the Lindenbaum quotient. Prove LindenbaumAlg is an STSA instance by wiring existing pieces (BooleanStructure, InteriorOperators, UltrafilterMCS). Restructure ParametricRepresentation into unified STSA representation theorem. Research report 001 provides complete algebraic analysis with ~80% of formalization already existing.
